objective: Create internal/generate/signal.go implementing the PRD §18.4 / reference_impl.md §1 signal contract for the snapshot-based atomic-commit pipeline: installSignalHandler(ctxCancel, rescueFn) arms a SIGINT/SIGTERM handler (post-snapshot) that cancels the executor context (process-group kill via Setpgid + grace SIGKILL), runs the rescue render if a snapshot was taken, and exits with ui.ExitRescue (3); restoreSignalHandler() resets SIGINT/SIGTERM to default and is called by CommitStaged immediately BEFORE UpdateRefCAS so a last-instant Ctrl-C is not mistaken for a failure. Deliver the file fully self-contained and unit/integration-tested, plus the two CommitStaged wiring call-sites applied only if generate.go already exists. This is a stdlib-only Go task (no testify), package generate, following the conventions of rescue.go/dedupe.go.
implementationSteps: CREATE internal/generate/signal.go (package generate; plain package line — generate.go owns the // Package doc comment like rescue.go/dedupe.go). Imports: os, os/signal, syscall, github.com/dustin/stagehand/internal/ui. Add the `var exit = os.Exit` injectable seam, `var commitSignals = []os.Signal{syscall.SIGINT, syscall.SIGTERM}`, and package-private `var activeCh chan os.Signal` (one active handler at a time).

In signal.go IMPLEMENT installSignalHandler(ctxCancel, rescueFn func()): make a buffered chan os.Signal(1); signal.Notify(ch, commitSignals...); store ch in activeCh; launch a goroutine that blocks on <-ch then does exit(handleSignal(ctxCancel, rescueFn)). Document: armed post-snapshot (after WriteTree, before generation) per PRD §18.4 / reference_impl.md §1.

In signal.go IMPLEMENT the pure core handleSignal(ctxCancel, rescueFn func()) int with NO os.Exit: call ctxCancel(); if rescueFn != nil { rescueFn() }; return ui.ExitRescue. This is the unit-testable seam (ctxCancel/rescueFn are caller closures; TREE gating lives in rescueFn).

In signal.go IMPLEMENT restoreSignalHandler(): if activeCh != nil { signal.Stop(activeCh); activeCh = nil }; then signal.Reset(commitSignals...). Document it is called by CommitStaged immediately BEFORE git.UpdateRefCAS (Gotcha #1) so a last-instant Ctrl-C is not mistaken for a failure.

CREATE internal/generate/signal_test.go (package generate white-box; stdlib bytes/io/os/os/exec/strings/testing + internal/ui ONLY; no testify). Add pure unit tests for handleSignal: (a) ctxCancel called + rescueFn called + returns ui.ExitRescue; (b) handleSignal(nil,nil) does not panic.

In signal_test.go ADD subprocess re-exec integration tests using the canonical Go pattern (child mode gated by an env var like STAGEHAND_SIGNAL_TEST). Test 1 (rescue on signal): child installs handler with a rescueFn writing the §18.3 block to os.Stderr then blocks; parent execs os.Args[0] -test.run with the env, sends SIGINT, asserts exit code == 3 AND stderr contains the rescue block + frozen TREE_SHA (use ui.NewOutput + Rescue, or a fake rescueFn — assert substring presence).

In signal_test.go ADD Test 2 (restore before update-ref): child installs handler, calls restoreSignalHandler(), runs a mocked update-ref (print a sentinel), exits 0; parent sends SIGINT during the mock; assert stderr has NO rescue block AND exit code != 3 (proves restore worked — default disposition, no rescue).

WIRE INTO CommitStaged ONLY IF internal/generate/generate.go already exists at implementation time: after WriteTree add installSignalHandler(cancel, func(){ if treeSHA != "" { Rescue(out, treeSHA, parentSHA, "") } }); immediately BEFORE g.UpdateRefCAS add restoreSignalHandler(). IF generate.go does NOT exist yet (P1.M6.T1.S1 not built), leave signal.go self-contained and document the exact 2-call-site snippet in a code comment / this PRP for T1.S1 — DO NOT create a stub CommitStaged (scope boundary).

RUN validation gates in order: go build ./internal/generate/ ; go vet ./internal/generate/ ; test -z "$(gofmt -l internal/generate/)" ; go test ./internal/generate/ -run HandleSignal -v ; go test ./internal/generate/ -run Signal -v ; go test ./internal/generate/. Fix any failure before declaring done.
Docs: this task has no Mode-A per-item DOCS line; it defers to the Mode-B changeset-level doc sync performed in M8. No doc file edit is required in this task.

successCriteria.description: internal/generate/signal.go exposes installSignalHandler(ctxCancel, rescueFn func()), restoreSignalHandler(), and a pure core handleSignal(...) int with an injectable `var exit = os.Exit` seam.
On SIGINT/SIGTERM the handler calls ctxCancel, then rescueFn, then exits ui.ExitRescue (3) — using the constant, not a hardcoded integer.
restoreSignalHandler() calls BOTH signal.Stop and signal.Reset to fully restore the default SIGINT/SIGTERM disposition (matches `trap - INT TERM`).

CommitStaged wiring restores the handler IMMEDIATELY BEFORE git.UpdateRefCAS (NOT after) — per the work-item contract and PRD §18.4 step 3; reference_impl.md §1 / decisions.md §3 line-positions are documented as superseded.
handleSignal is unit-tested with NO os.Exit (asserts ctxCancel + rescueFn invoked and returns ui.ExitRescue); handleSignal(nil,nil) does not panic.
A subprocess integration test proves a real SIGINT to a handler-armed process prints the §18.3 rescue block (containing the frozen TREE_SHA) and exits 3.
A subprocess integration test proves that after restoreSignalHandler(), a SIGINT during a mocked update-ref does NOT print rescue and the path completes (exit code != 3).
go build, go vet, gofmt, and go test ./internal/generate/ all pass; no testify / external deps added; signal.go uses a plain `package generate` line (generate.go owns the package doc comment).
Scope respected: generate.go is left untouched unless CommitStaged (P1.M6.T1.S1) already exists; no stub CommitStaged is created in this task.
Docs: task has no Mode-A DOCS line; defers to Mode-B changeset-level doc sync in M8 — no doc file edit required here.

references: internal/generate/rescue.go — Rescue(out, tree, parent, candidate) PURE RENDER; candidate="" on signal path; Rescue never os.Exit
internal/generate/rescue_test.go — package generate white-box test conventions; ui.NewOutput(io.Discard,&stderr,false,true); containsAll helper
internal/provider/executor.go — Executor.Run(ctx,...) ctx.Done() → SIGTERM+SIGKILL process group (Setpgid); ctx parameter is the cancel seam
internal/ui/output.go — NewOutput(stdout,stderr,verbose,noColor); Progressf (stderr ALWAYS, FR51); Red(s)
plan/001_f1f80943ac34/architecture/reference_impl.md §1 (trap after write-tree), §5 (trap - INT TERM), §7 (signal handling is NEW PRD work)
plan/001_f1f80943ac34/architecture/decisions.md §3 (CommitStaged pseudocode COMMIT block; restore comment text says BEFORE update-ref)
PRD.md §18.2 (SIGINT/SIGTERM post-snapshot → rescue, exit 3), §18.3 (rescue block), §18.4 (Notify SIGINT+SIGTERM; cancel+kill group; rescue if snapshot taken; restore before update-ref)
https://pkg.go.dev/os/signal#Stop — signal.Stop stops delivery to the channel
